item.title: Doctor Web’s Q2 2026 review of virus activity on mobile devices
item.description: Source: Dr.Web / Source attributes: Russia / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Malware, Phishing, Threat Actor, Mobile, Endpoint, Detection / Topics: Android, Microsoft, Malware, Phishing / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, EN critical sector, campaign priority, IOC 57, linked IOC evidence, target and impact context, detection evidence context, critical sector, primary references 1 / Summary: July 1, 2026 According to detection statistics collected by Dr.Web Security Space for mobile devices, Android.Banker banking trojans were less active in the second quarter (Q2) of 2026—by 31.83%, compared to the first quarter (Q1). At the same time, they remained the most widespread Android malware: this family accounted for 23.28% of all malware detections. These trojans steal various confidential data, such as SMS containing one-time codes from credit organizations and logins and passwords for accessing online bank accounts, and they can also display phishing windows. As in the previous quar / Primary source: Dr.Web https://news.drweb.com/show/?i=15274&lng=en&c=5 / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: Doctor Web’s Q2 2026 virus activity review
item.description: Source: Dr.Web / Source attributes: Russia / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Vulnerability, Exploit, Malware, Ransomware, Phishing, Threat Actor, Mobile, Endpoint, Detection, Cryptocurrency / Topics: Android, Apple/iOS, Microsoft, Ransomware, Vulnerability, Exploit, Malware, Phishing / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, EN critical sector, campaign priority, IOC 3, linked IOC evidence, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, critical sector, primary references 1 / Summary: July 1, 2026 According to statistics collected by the Dr.Web anti-virus, the total number of threats detected in the second quarter (Q2) of 2026 was up by 5.72% from Q1. The number of unique threats increased by 95.32%. Ad-displaying programs and trojans, malicious crypto-miners, and trojans used to run other malware were most frequently discovered on protected devices. In email traffic, malicious scripts and different types of trojans, such as downloaders, droppers, password stealers, and miners, were most regularly detected. Also commonly encountered were backdoors, phishing documents, and v / Primary source: Dr.Web https://news.drweb.com/show/?i=15275&lng=en&c=5 / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: Glitch SPY: An Emerging Android RAT Distributed Through a Fake Polish Rental App
item.description: Source: Cyble / Source attributes: Singapore / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Malware, Threat Intel, Threat Actor, Supply Chain, Identity, Mobile, Cryptocurrency / Topics: Android, Supply Chain, Malware, Identity / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, campaign priority, IOC 3, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, indicator context, primary references 1 / Summary: Executive Summary Cyble Research and Intelligence Labs identified an emerging Android malware family tracked as Glitch SPY , distributed through a fraudulent Polish apartment and house rental platform designed to lure users into downloading an Android APK. Based on the Polish-language lure and rental-themed distribution website, the activity appears to be Poland-focused, targeting users in Poland or Polish expats. The downloaded application functions as a dropper and installs the Glitch SPY payload after convincing the user to allow installation from unknown sources. Glitch SPY prompts the vic / Primary source: Cyble https://cyble.com/blog/glitch-spy-rat-distributed-via-fake-polish-app/ / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: 【2026年5月 マルウェアレポート】正規の生成AI共有リンクを悪用するLLMShareの仕組みと対策
item.description: Source: Canon MJ ESET Security / Source attributes: Japan / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, Vulnerability, Exploit, Ransomware, Phishing, Threat Actor, Data Breach, Mobile, AI Security, Endpoint / Topics: Android, Apple/iOS, Linux, Microsoft, Ransomware, Malware, Vulnerability, Exploit, Phishing, Data Breach, AI Security / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (95) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, JA attack activity, JA active exploit/PoC/zero-day, JA detection/analysis, JA Japan/Asia relevance, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, Japan related, primary references 1 / Summary: 5月の概況 2026年5月（5月1日～5月31日）にESET製品が国内で検出したマルウェアの検出数の推移は、以下のとおりです。 国内マルウェア検出数*1の推移 (2025年12月の検出数を100%として比較) *1 検出数にはPUA（Potentially Unwanted/Unsafe Application; 必ずしも悪意があるとは限らないが、コンピューターのパフォーマンスに悪影響を及ぼす可能性があるアプリケーション）を含めています。以降のグラフにはPUAが含まれています。 2026年5月の国内マルウェア検出数は、2026年4月と比較して増加しました。検出されたマルウェアの内訳は以下のとおりです。 国内マルウェア検出数*2上位（2026年5月） 順位 マルウェア名 割合 種別 1 DOC/Fraud 48.1% 詐欺サイトのリンクが埋め込まれたDOCファイル 2 JS/Adware.Agent 12.5% アドウェア 3 HTML/Phishing.Agent 5.4% メールに添付された不正なHTMLファイル 4 JS/Agent 2.4% 不正なJavaScriptの汎用検出名 5 Win32/Exploit.CVE-2017-0199 1.6% 脆弱性CVE-2017-0199を悪用するプログラム 6 JS/Danger.ScriptAttachment 1.4% ダウン / Primary source: Canon MJ ESET Security https://eset-info.canon-its.jp/malware_info/malware_topics/detail/malware2605.html / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: How Agentic AI Enables Autonomous Threat Response at Machine Speed
item.description: Source: Ivanti Security / Source attributes: United States / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Security, Vulnerability, Threat Intel, Exploit, Zero-Day, Patch, Threat Actor, Identity, Data Breach, AI Security, Endpoint, Network, Detection, Incident Response / Topics: Ivanti, Microsoft, Network Edge, Vulnerability, Exploit, Zero-Day, Identity, Data Breach, AI Security / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN active exploit/PoC/zero-day, EN detection/analysis, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, primary references 1 / Summary: Why do 40% of alerts received by security teams today go completely uninvestigated? It’s not due to a lack of concern but instead caused by shortening attack windows and compounded by overwhelming tech sprawl. Today’s security teams are operating in a threat landscape defined by escalating attacks, tighter budgets and mounting alert fatigue. Organizations process an average of 960 security alerts per day , and large enterprises handle more than 3,000 daily alerts across roughly 30 tools. That adds up to 36,000 potential threats a month that could slip through the cracks. The asymmetry is crush / Primary source: Ivanti Security https://www.ivanti.com/blog/agentic-ai-autonomous-threat-response / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: The Bear Necessities: A Look at the Drivers, Dynamics, and Applications of the Pro-Russia Influence Ecosystem
item.description: Source: Google Cloud Threat Intelligence / Source attributes: United States / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, APT, Cloud, AI Security, Threat Actor / Topics: APT, Cloud, AI Security / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: APT / targeted campaign (rank 6) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: APT / targeted campaign, EN detection/analysis, EN targeted/APT/state-backed, EN critical sector, APT/campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, critical sector, primary references 1 / Summary: Written by: James Sadowski, Alden Wahlstrom Introduction Four years into Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, the pro-Russia influence ecosystem has evolved from a tool of war back into a global strategic asset. Since the mobilization of this ecosystem to support frontline objectives, we have witnessed the expedited development of new influence assets linked to multiple, expansive, covert information operations (IO) campaigns and a revitalization of pro-Russia hacktivism at an unprecedented scale. While this threat activity initially adapted to encompass Ukraine-related priorities, it is g / Primary source: Google Cloud Threat Intelligence https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/threat-intelligence/pro-russia-influence-ecosystem/ / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: From Bing Search to Ransomware: Bumblebee and AdaptixC2 Deliver Akira

This report contains data from both intrusions. We plan to release a DFIR Labs […] The post From Bing Search to Ransomware: Bumblebee and AdaptixC2 Deliver Akira appeared first on The DFIR Report . / Primary source: DFIR Report https://thedfirreport.com/2026/06/29/from-bing-search-to-ransomware-bumblebee-and-adaptixc2-deliver-akira-3/ / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: Modernizing Global Vulnerability Standards For The Age Of AI
item.description: Source: Rapid7 / Source attributes: United States / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Research, Vulnerability, Exploit, AI Security / Topics: Vulnerability, Exploit, AI Security / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN active exploit/PoC/zero-day, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, vulnerability exploitation context, primary references 1 / Summary: As AI-driven vulnerability discovery accelerates, the cybersecurity ecosystem is being forced to examine whether the standards, disclosure processes, and prioritization frameworks defenders rely on can still keep pace. Many of those systems were built around human-speed discovery, manageable vulnerability volumes, and exploitability confirmed after the fact, which leaves them under increasing strain as frontier AI capabilities mature. During a private sector consultation with the White House in June, Corey Thomas and I presented Rapid7’s new policy paper, Modernizing Global Vulnerability Stand / Primary source: Rapid7 https://www.rapid7.com/blog/post/ai-modernizing-global-vulnerability-standards / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: Operation Endgame Disrupts SocGholish, StealC Malware Networks
item.description: Source: The Cyber Express / Source attributes: Global / News / Secondary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, Ransomware, Phishing, Threat Actor, AI Security, Network, Cryptocurrency / Topics: Microsoft, Network Edge, Ransomware, Malware, Phishing, AI Security / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (83) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN critical sector, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, indicator context, critical sector, primary references 2 / Summary: Operation Endgame has dealt another blow to cybercriminal operations after international law enforcement agencies and private sector partners dismantled infrastructure supporting the SocGholish, Amadey, and StealC malware families. The coordinated operation resulted in the seizure of more than EUR 41 million in criminal cryptocurrency assets, the recovery of 27 million stolen login credentials , and the disruption of hundreds of servers and domains used to distribute malware. Led by Europol and Eurojust, the operation brought together authorities from Canada, Denmark, Germany, the Netherlands, / Primary source: europol.europa.eu https://www.europol.europa.eu/media-press/newsroom/news/global-cyber-strike-disrupts-socgholish-amadey-and-stealc-malware-networks

item.title: Government Website in India Taken Offline After Defacement Attack
item.description: Source: The Cyber Express / Source attributes: Global / News / Secondary / Categories: Threat Intel, Vulnerability, Data Breach, Network, Incident Response / Topics: Network Edge, Vulnerability, Data Breach / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: Medium (67) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, EN critical sector, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, indicator context, critical sector, primary references 1 / Summary: A government website in northern India was taken offline after unidentified hackers allegedly gained unauthorized access and replaced its homepage with pro-Pakistan messages and slogans. The MDA hack targeted the official website of the Meerut Development Authority, a government agency responsible for urban development in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. Authorities discovered the breach on Saturday and immediately disabled public access to the website while technical teams worked to contain the incident and restore services. Instead of the agency's usual information on urban planning, housi / Primary source: Cyble https://cyble.com/cyberattack/

item.title: ATM Jackpotting Gang Members Sentenced for Ploutus Malware Attacks
item.description: Source: The Cyber Express / Source attributes: Global / News / Secondary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, Ransomware, Network / Topics: Network Edge, Ransomware, Malware / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (83) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN critical sector, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, critical sector, primary references 2 / Summary: Two Venezuelan nationals have been sentenced to 78 months in prison for their role in an ATM jackpotting scheme that used malware to force cash machines across the United States to dispense money illegally. The operation, which authorities say was part of a broader transnational criminal network, involved the deployment of Ploutus malware on ATMs and resulted in losses exceeding $1.5 million. Carlos Javier Padron, 36, was sentenced after pleading guilty to conspiracy to commit bank burglary and computer fraud. His co-defendant, Oddry Arnoldo Cabrera Torrealba, 37, received the same sentence on / Primary source: Cyble https://cyble.com/knowledge-hub/what-is-malware/

item.title: Critical Unauthenticated Remote Code Execution in Splunk Enterprise (CVE-2026-20253)
item.description: Source: Zscaler / Source attributes: United States / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Vulnerability, Exploit, Patch, Cloud, Identity, Endpoint, Network, Detection / Topics: CVE-2026-20253, Cloud Security, Vulnerability, Exploit, Cloud, Identity / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN active exploit/PoC/zero-day, EN detection/analysis, JA active exploit/PoC/zero-day, campaign priority, IOC 1, linked IOC evidence, coherent attack event, target and impact context, vulnerability exploitation context, indicator context, primary references 1 / Summary: IntroductionSplunk disclosed a critical unauthenticated remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in Splunk Enterprise tracked as CVE-2026-20253 on June 10, 2026. The vulnerability has a CVSS score of 9.8 and stems from missing authentication on a PostgreSQL sidecar service recovery endpoint that can be reached through the Splunk Web interface, which proxies requests to the internal PostgreSQL sidecar service without enforcing authentication. A successful attacker can create or truncate arbitrary files and ultimately achieve arbitrary code execution under the Splunk service account.On June 12, / Primary source: Zscaler https://www.zscaler.com/blogs/security-research/critical-unauthenticated-remote-code-execution-splunk-enterprise-cve-2026 / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: CVE-2026-20245 Zero-Day Exploited in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager to Gain Root Access
item.description: Source: The Cyber Express / Source attributes: Global / News / Secondary / Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Actor, Threat Intel, Exploit, Zero-Day, APT, Network, Detection, Incident Response / Topics: CVE-2026-20245, Cisco, Network Edge, Vulnerability, Exploit, Zero-Day, APT / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (91) / Focus: APT / targeted campaign (rank 6) / Reference count: 2 / Reasons: focus: APT / targeted campaign, EN attack activity, EN IOC/detection/hunting, EN active exploit/PoC/zero-day, EN detection/analysis, EN targeted/APT/state-backed, APT/campaign priority, IOC 15, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, indicator context, reported by 2 sources, primary references 1 / Summary: A newly disclosed zero-day vulnerability, CVE-2026-20245, has been exploited by a threat actor targeting Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager. By exploiting a flaw in the platform's file to upload functionality, the threat actor escalated privileges from a compromised administrative account to root access and used extensive anti-forensic measures to erase evidence of the attack. Threat Actor Abused Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager to Gain Root Access Mandiant found that the threat actor initially established unauthorized peering connections before accessing Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager over SSH. In Mar / Also reported by: The HackersNews / Primary source: Cyble https://cyble.com/exploit/

item.title: 国内ISPの認証情報を不正利用したフィッシングメールの注意喚起
item.description: Source: Canon MJ ESET Security / Source attributes: Japan / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, Phishing, Mobile / Topics: Malware, Phishing / Priority: High (67) / SOC: 55 / Confidence: High (81) / Focus: incident / breach (rank 3) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: incident / breach, JA attack activity, JA detection/analysis, JA Japan/Asia relevance, uncertain reporting, Japan related, primary references 1 / Summary: 2026年6月26日現在、国内ISPの認証情報（ID、メールアドレス、パスワード等）を不正利用（乗っ取り）して送信されたとみられるフィッシングが確認されております。 このようなフィッシングサイトにて、情報（メールアドレス、電話番号等）を入力しないよう、ご注意ください。 ご利用のISPやメールサービスのホームページ上の注意喚起やニュース等を確認し、漏えい対象のサービスをお使いの場合は、メールアカウントが乗っ取られる可能性があるため、速やかにサービスのパスワード変更等を行ってください。 もしご利用のサービスからメール等で連絡が来た場合は、メール等のリンクからではなく、すでにご利用のアプリやブラウザーのブックマークからサービスへアクセスして設定するなど、便乗したフィッシングにご注意ください。 【メールの件名】 【重要】VISA力一ド ご利用制限のお知らせ 【重要】お客様のAmazonアカウントが一時的に制限されています 【重要】アカウントが一時的に制限されています 【Amazon.co.jp】お支払い方法の確認が必要です — アカウント利用制限中 【Amazon.co.jp】お荷物の配送に問題が発生しました お荷物の配送状況に関するご連絡 お荷物の配送に問題が発生しました 荷物のお届けに問題がございます お届け予定の荷物に問題が見つかりました お届け中の荷物に異常が確認されました ※ 上 / Primary source: Canon MJ ESET Security https://eset-info.canon-its.jp/malware_info/news/detail/260630.html / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: What Is Attack Surface Management (ASM)?
item.description: Source: SOCRadar Blog / Source attributes: Global / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Research, IOC, Vulnerability, Exploit, Cloud / Topics: Vulnerability, Exploit, Cloud / Priority: High (67) / SOC: 43 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, campaign priority, coherent attack event, indicator context, primary references 1 / Summary: What Is Attack Surface Management (ASM)? Attack Surface Management (ASM) is the continuous discovery, monitoring, and prioritization of an organization’s exposed digital assets, including cloud infrastructure, SaaS applications, internet-facing systems, identities, and third-party integrations. The goal is to identify and reduce vulnerabilities before attackers can exploit them. Modern environments change faster than traditional security processes […] / Primary source: SOCRadar Blog https://socradar.io/blog/what-is-attack-surface-management/ / Primary source age: 0 days
